title: Syracuse common council stands by budget cuts as mayor decides his next move
summary: The Syracuse Common Council unanimously approved $16 million in budget cuts across city departments, eliminating a proposed property tax increase and reducing reserve draws. Acting Council President Pat Hogan defended the reductions despite criticism from the Walsh administration, stating the council remains firm on its proposal while remaining open to discussion. The mayor has until Monday to accept, reject, or negotiate the council's amendments, with a final budget deadline of early June.

political_signal.description: Council has adopted antagonistic budget position contrary to mayor's proposal; Hogan's mayoral candidacy creates potential conflict of interest; department heads expressing inability to meet constituent service expectations under cuts
